message:
test(fuzz): add CBOR indefinite-length wire-form rejection fuzz (resolves d14ee)

CBOR has indefinite-length encodings (RFC 8949 §3.2): 0x9f for array,
0xbf for map, 0x5f for byte string, 0x7f for text string, all
terminated by 0xff break. Canonical CBOR (§4.2.1) mandates definite-
length only.

CanonicalSerializer::deserialize re-encodes the decoded Value via
to_canonical_cbor (which emits definite-length) and compares to input
bytes — indefinite-length input should fail with NonCanonicalEncoding.

Existing dyfmr (decode_hello_ack_canonical) and cchj5 cover trailing-
bytes and schema-prefix gates but NOT the indefinite-length rejection.
A regression that accepted indefinite-length wire forms would let
attackers smuggle non-canonical bytes past the canonical-encoding gate.

One property driven over hand-constructed indefinite-length wire forms
(arrays/maps/byte-strings/text-strings) selected by discriminator —
each MUST be rejected with a typed error from the cbor family
(NonCanonicalEncoding / CborDeserialize / PayloadTooLarge /
TrailingBytes).

Once-gated anchors verify each canonical indefinite-length wire form:
- 0x9f 0x00 0xff (indef array containing int 0)
- 0xbf 0x00 0xf6 0xff (indef map: 0 → null)
- 0x5f 0x40 0xff (indef byte string with empty chunk)
- 0x7f 0x60 0xff (indef text string with empty chunk)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
